revision.comment: 新規立項（[[奈良時代]]の[[皇族]]）
revision.text:
'''河内王'''（かわちおう、生没年不詳）は、[[奈良時代]]の[[皇族]]。[[官位]]は[[正五位|正五位下]]・[[丹後国#国司|丹後守]]。
== 経歴 == [[聖武天皇|聖武朝]]の[[天平]]9年（[[737年]]）[[无位]]から[[従五位|従五位下]]に直叙される。
[[天平宝字]]2年（[[758年]]）[[淳仁天皇]]の[[即位]]に際して、[[奉幣]]を行うために、[[散位]]・[[中臣池守]]らとともに[[伊勢神宮|伊勢太神宮]]に派遣された（この時の[[官職]]は[[大舎人寮|左大舎人頭]]）<ref>『続日本紀』天平宝字2年8月19日条</ref>。天平宝字3年（[[759年]]）22年振りに昇叙されて従五位上になると、天平宝字4年（[[760年]]）[[刑部省|義部大輔]]、天平宝字6年（[[762年]]）[[丹後国#国司|丹後守]]に任ぜられている。しかし、天平宝字8年（764年）に発生した[[藤原仲麻呂の乱]]に[[連座]]したらしく[[官位]]を剥奪される。
[[宝亀]]元年（[[770年]]）[[光仁天皇]]の即位に伴って従五位上に復されると、宝亀3年（[[772年]]）には[[正五位|正五位下]]に叙せられた。
== 官歴 == 『[[続日本紀]]』による。 *[[天平]]9年（[[737年]]） 9月28日：[[従五位|従五位下]]（[[蔭位|直叙]]） *[[天平宝字]]2年（[[758年]]） 8月19日：見[[大舎人寮|左大舎人頭]] *天平宝字3年（[[759年]]） 6月16日：従五位上 *天平宝字4年（[[760年]]） 正月16日：[[刑部省|義部大輔]] *天平宝字6年（[[762年]]） 正月9日：[[丹後国#国司|丹後守]] *時期不詳：[[官位]]剥奪 *[[宝亀]]元年（[[770年]]） 10月1日：従五位上（復本位） *宝亀3年（[[772年]]） 正月3日：[[正五位|正五位下]]
== 脚注 == {{reflist|2}}
== 参考文献 == *[[宇治谷孟]]『続日本紀 （上）』[[講談社]]〈[[講談社学術文庫]]〉、1992年 *宇治谷孟『続日本紀 （中）』講談社〈講談社学術文庫〉、1992年 *宇治谷孟『続日本紀 （下）』講談社〈講談社学術文庫〉、1995年
{{DEFAULTSORT:かわちおう}} [[Category:奈良時代の男性皇族]] [[Category:8世紀生]] [[Category:8世紀没]]

'''河内王'''（かわちおう、生没年不詳）は、[[奈良時代]]の[[皇族]]。[[官位]]は[[正五位|正五位下]]・[[丹後国#国司|丹後守]]。
== 経歴 == [[聖武天皇|聖武朝]]の[[天平]]9年（[[737年]]）[[无位]]から[[従五位|従五位下]]に直叙される。
[[天平宝字]]2年（[[758年]]）[[淳仁天皇]]の[[即位]]に際して、[[奉幣]]を行うために、[[散位]]・[[中臣池守]]らとともに[[伊勢神宮|伊勢太神宮]]に派遣された（この時の[[官職]]は[[大舎人寮|左大舎人頭]]）<ref>『続日本紀』天平宝字2年8月19日条</ref>。天平宝字3年（[[759年]]）22年振りに昇叙されて従五位上になると、天平宝字4年（[[760年]]）[[刑部省|義部大輔]]、天平宝字6年（[[762年]]）[[丹後国#国司|丹後守]]に任ぜられている。しかし、天平宝字8年（[[764年]]）に発生した[[藤原仲麻呂の乱]]に[[連座]]したらしく[[官位]]を剥奪される。
[[宝亀]]元年（[[770年]]）[[光仁天皇]]の即位に伴って従五位上に復されると、宝亀3年（[[772年]]）には[[正五位|正五位下]]に叙せられた。
== 官歴 == 『[[続日本紀]]』による。 *[[天平]]9年（[[737年]]） 9月28日：[[従五位|従五位下]]（[[蔭位|直叙]]） *[[天平宝字]]2年（[[758年]]） 8月19日：見[[大舎人寮|左大舎人頭]] *天平宝字3年（[[759年]]） 6月16日：従五位上 *天平宝字4年（[[760年]]） 正月16日：[[刑部省|義部大輔]] *天平宝字6年（[[762年]]） 正月9日：[[丹後国#国司|丹後守]] *時期不詳：[[官位]]剥奪 *[[宝亀]]元年（[[770年]]） 10月1日：従五位上（復本位） *宝亀3年（[[772年]]） 正月3日：[[正五位|正五位下]]
== 脚注 == {{reflist|2}}
== 参考文献 == *[[宇治谷孟]]『続日本紀 （上）』[[講談社]]〈[[講談社学術文庫]]〉、1992年 *宇治谷孟『続日本紀 （中）』講談社〈講談社学術文庫〉、1992年 *宇治谷孟『続日本紀 （下）』講談社〈講談社学術文庫〉、1995年
{{DEFAULTSORT:かわちおう}} [[Category:奈良時代の男性皇族]] [[Category:8世紀生]] [[Category:8世紀没]]

[[宝亀]]元年（[[770年]]）[[光仁天皇]]の即位に伴って従五位上に復されると、宝亀3年（[[772年]]）には[[正五位|正五位下]]に叙せられた。

== 参考文献 == *[[宇治谷孟]]『続日本紀 （上）』[[講談社]]〈[[講談社学術文庫]]〉、1992年 *宇治谷孟『続日本紀 （中）』講談社〈講談社学術文庫〉、1992年 *宇治谷孟『続日本紀 （下）』講談社〈講談社学術文庫〉、1995年

'''河内王'''（かわちおう、生没年不詳）は、[[奈良時代]]の[[皇族]]。[[官位]]は[[正五位|正五位下]]・[[丹後国#国司|丹後守]]。
== 経歴 == [[聖武天皇|聖武朝]]の[[天平]]9年（[[737年]]）[[无位]]から[[従五位|従五位下]]に直叙される。
[[天平宝字]]2年（[[758年]]）[[淳仁天皇]]の[[即位]]に際して、[[奉幣]]を行うために、[[散位]]・[[中臣池守]]らとともに[[伊勢神宮|伊勢太神宮]]に派遣された（この時の[[官職]]は[[大舎人寮|左大舎人頭]]）<ref>『続日本紀』天平宝字2年8月19日条</ref>。天平宝字3年（[[759年]]）22年振りに昇叙されて従五位上になると、天平宝字4年（[[760年]]）[[刑部省|義部大輔]]、天平宝字6年（[[762年]]）[[丹後国#国司|丹後守]]に任ぜられている。しかし、天平宝字8年（[[764年]]）に発生した[[藤原仲麻呂の乱]]に[[連座]]したらしく[[官位]]を剥奪される。

== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}}

https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/%E6%B2%B3%E5%86%85%E7%8E%8B_(%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E6%99%82%E4%BB%A3%E5%BE%8C%E6%9C%9F)
[[Category:two pages]]

[[Category:https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki]][[ja:%E6%B2%B3%E5%86%85%E7%8E%8B_(%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E6%99%82%E4%BB%A3%E5%BE%8C%E6%9C%9F)
[[Category:two pages]]
[[Category:https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki]][[ja:%E6%B2%B3%E5%86%85%E7%8E%8B_(%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E6%99%82%E4%BB%A3%E5%BE%8C%E6%9C%9F)]][[Category:two pages]]

[[Category:https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki]][[ja:%E6%B2%B3%E5%86%85%E7%8E%8B_(%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E6%99%82%E4%BB%A3%E5%BE%8C%E6%9C%9F)]][[Category:two pages]] '''Kawachi Ō''' (河内王, dates of birth and death unknown) was a {{ill|Prince of Japan|ja|日本の王 (皇族)}} during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. He held the rank of {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正五位下}} and served as Governor of {{ill|Tango Province|ja|丹後国}}.
== Career == In the {{ill|Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}} court during the ninth year of the {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} era (737), Kawachi Ō was directly promoted from no rank to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下}}.
In the second year of the {{ill|Tenpyō Hōji|ja|天平宝字}} era (758), during the enthronement of Emperor {{ill|Junnin|ja|淳仁天皇}}, Kawachi Ō was dispatched to perform rituals at the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}} alongside {{ill|Nakatomi no Ikemori|ja|中臣池守}} and others. At the time, his official position was {{ill|Head of the Left Bureau of Imperial Attendants|ja|左大舎人頭}}.<ref>『続日本紀』天平宝字2年8月19日条</ref>
In the third year of the Tenpyō Hōji era (759), he received his first promotion in 22 years to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上}}. In the fourth year (760), he was appointed as {{ill|Assistant Minister of Justice|ja|義部大輔}}, and in the sixth year (762), he became Governor of Tango Province. However, he appears to have been implicated in the {{ill|Fujiwara no Nakamaro Rebellion|ja|藤原仲麻呂の乱}} in the eighth year (764), resulting in the loss of his rank and position.
In the first year of the {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀}} era (770), upon the enthronement of Emperor {{ill|Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇}}, he was reinstated to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. In the third year (772), he was promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade.
== Official Record == Based on the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}: * {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} 9 (737), September 28: Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (direct promotion) * Tenpyō Hōji 2 (758), August 19: Left Bureau of Imperial Attendants (current position) * Tenpyō Hōji 3 (759), June 16: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade * Tenpyō Hōji 4 (760), January 16: Assistant Minister of Justice * Tenpyō Hōji 6 (762), January 9: Governor of Tango Province * Date unknown: Rank and position forfeited * {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀}} 1 (770), October 1: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (reinstatement) * Hōki 3 (772), January 3: Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}}
== References == * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟}}, *Shoku Nihongi (Volume 1)*, {{ill|Kodansha|ja|講談社}}, Kodansha Academic Library, 1992. * Ujitani Mitsuru, *Shoku Nihongi (Volume 2)*, Kodansha, Kodansha Academic Library, 1992. * Ujitani Mitsuru, *Shoku Nihongi (Volume 3)*, Kodansha, Kodansha Academic Library, 1995.
[[Category:Princes of Japan]] [[Category:Male royals of the Nara period]] [[Category:Recipients of Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] [[Category:8th-century births]] [[Category:8th-century deaths]] [[ja:河内王]]

revision.text:
[[Category:https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[ja:%E6%B2%B3%E5%86%85%E7%8E%8B_(%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E6%99%82%E4%BB%A3%E5%BE%8C%E6%9C%9F)]][[Category:Two pages]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] '''Kawachi Ō''' (河内王, dates of birth and death unknown) was a {{ill|Prince of Japan|ja|日本の王 (皇族)}} during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. He held the rank of {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正五位下}} and served as Governor of {{ill|Tango Province|ja|丹後国}}.
== Career == In the {{ill|Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}} court during the ninth year of the {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} era (737), Kawachi Ō was directly promoted from no rank to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下}}.
In the second year of the {{ill|Tenpyō Hōji|ja|天平宝字}} era (758), during the enthronement of Emperor {{ill|Junnin|ja|淳仁天皇}}, Kawachi Ō was dispatched to perform rituals at the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}} alongside {{ill|Nakatomi no Ikemori|ja|中臣池守}} and others. At the time, his official position was {{ill|Head of the Left Bureau of Imperial Attendants|ja|左大舎人頭}}.<ref>『続日本紀』天平宝字2年8月19日条</ref>
In the third year of the Tenpyō Hōji era (759), he received his first promotion in 22 years to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上}}. In the fourth year (760), he was appointed as {{ill|Assistant Minister of Justice|ja|義部大輔}}, and in the sixth year (762), he became Governor of Tango Province. However, he appears to have been implicated in the {{ill|Fujiwara no Nakamaro Rebellion|ja|藤原仲麻呂の乱}} in the eighth year (764), resulting in the loss of his rank and position.
In the first year of the {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀}} era (770), upon the enthronement of Emperor {{ill|Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇}}, he was reinstated to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. In the third year (772), he was promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade.
== Official Record == Based on the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}: * {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} 9 (737), September 28: Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (direct promotion) * Tenpyō Hōji 2 (758), August 19: Left Bureau of Imperial Attendants (current position) * Tenpyō Hōji 3 (759), June 16: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade * Tenpyō Hōji 4 (760), January 16: Assistant Minister of Justice * Tenpyō Hōji 6 (762), January 9: Governor of Tango Province * Date unknown: Rank and position forfeited * {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀}} 1 (770), October 1: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (reinstatement) * Hōki 3 (772), January 3: Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}}
== References == * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟}}, *Shoku Nihongi (Volume 1)*, {{ill|Kodansha|ja|講談社}}, Kodansha Academic Library, 1992. * Ujitani Mitsuru, *Shoku Nihongi (Volume 2)*, Kodansha, Kodansha Academic Library, 1992. * Ujitani Mitsuru, *Shoku Nihongi (Volume 3)*, Kodansha, Kodansha Academic Library, 1995.

[[Category:https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[ja:%E6%B2%B3%E5%86%85%E7%8E%8B_(%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E6%99%82%E4%BB%A3%E5%BE%8C%E6%9C%9F)]][[Category:Two pages]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] '''Kawachi Ō''' (河内王, dates of birth and death unknown) was a {{ill|Prince of Japan|ja|日本の王 (皇族)}} during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. He held the rank of {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正五位下}} and served as Governor of {{ill|Tango Province|ja|丹後国}}.
== Career == In the {{ill|Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}} court during the ninth year of the {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} era (737), Kawachi Ō was directly promoted from no rank to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下}}.
In the second year of the {{ill|Tenpyō Hōji|ja|天平宝字}} era (758), during the enthronement of Emperor {{ill|Junnin|ja|淳仁天皇}}, Kawachi Ō was dispatched to perform rituals at the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}} alongside {{ill|Nakatomi no Ikemori|ja|中臣池守}} and others. At the time, his official position was {{ill|Head of the Left Bureau of Imperial Attendants|ja|左大舎人頭}}.<ref>『続日本紀』天平宝字2年8月19日条</ref>
In the third year of the Tenpyō Hōji era (759), he received his first promotion in 22 years to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上}}. In the fourth year (760), he was appointed as {{ill|Assistant Minister of Justice|ja|義部大輔}}, and in the sixth year (762), he became Governor of Tango Province. However, he appears to have been implicated in the {{ill|Fujiwara no Nakamaro Rebellion|ja|藤原仲麻呂の乱}} in the eighth year (764), resulting in the loss of his rank and position.
In the first year of the {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀}} era (770), upon the enthronement of Emperor {{ill|Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇}}, he was reinstated to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. In the third year (772), he was promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade.
== Official Record == Based on the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}: * {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} 9 (737), September 28: Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (direct promotion) * Tenpyō Hōji 2 (758), August 19: Left Bureau of Imperial Attendants (current position) * Tenpyō Hōji 3 (759), June 16: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade * Tenpyō Hōji 4 (760), January 16: Assistant Minister of Justice * Tenpyō Hōji 6 (762), January 9: Governor of Tango Province * Date unknown: Rank and position forfeited * {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀}} 1 (770), October 1: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (reinstatement) * Hōki 3 (772), January 3: Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}}
== References == * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟}}, *Shoku Nihongi (Volume 1)*, {{ill|Kodansha|ja|講談社}}, Kodansha Academic Library, 1992. * Ujitani Mitsuru, *Shoku Nihongi (Volume 2)*, Kodansha, Kodansha Academic Library, 1992. * Ujitani Mitsuru, *Shoku Nihongi (Volume 3)*, Kodansha, Kodansha Academic Library, 1995.

[[ja:%E6%B2%B3%E5%86%85%E7%8E%8B_(%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E6%99%82%E4%BB%A3%E5%BE%8C%E6%9C%9F)]][[Category:Two pages]]
'''Kawachi Ō''' (河内王, dates of birth and death unknown) was a {{ill|Prince of Japan|ja|日本の王 (皇族)}} during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. He held the rank of {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正五位下}} and served as Governor of {{ill|Tango Province|ja|丹後国}}.
== Career == In the court of [[Emperor Shōmu]] during the ninth year of the {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} era (737), Kawachi Ō was directly promoted from no rank to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下}}.
In the second year of the {{ill|Tenpyō Hōji|ja|天平宝字}} era (758), during the enthronement of Emperor {{ill|Junnin|ja|淳仁天皇}}, Kawachi Ō was dispatched to perform rituals at the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}} alongside {{ill|Nakatomi no Ikemori|ja|中臣池守}} and others. At the time, his official position was {{ill|Head of the Left Bureau of Imperial Attendants|ja|左大舎人頭}}.<ref>『続日本紀』天平宝字2年8月19日条</ref>
In the third year of the Tenpyō Hōji era (759), he received his first promotion in 22 years to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ltt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上}}. In the fourth year (760), he was appointed as {{ill|Assistant Minister of Justice|ja|義部大輔}}, and in the sixth year (762), he became Governor of Tango Province. However, he appears to have been implicated in the {{ill|Fujiwara no Nakamaro Rebellion|ja|藤原仲麻呂の乱}} in the eighth year (764), resulting in the loss of his rank and position.
In the first year of the {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀}} era (770), upon the enthronement of Emperor {{ill|Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇}}, he was reinstated to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. In the third year (772), he was promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade.
== Official Record == Based on the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}: * {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} 9 (737), September 28: Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (direct promotion) * Tenpyō Hōji 2 (758), August 19: Left Bureau of Imperial Attendants (current position) * Tenpyō Hōji 3 (759), June 16: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade * Tenpyō Hōji 4 (760), January 16: Assistant Minister of Justice * Tenpyō Hōji 6 (762), January 9: Governor of Tango Province * Date unknown: Rank and position forfeited * {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀}} 1 (770), October 1: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (reinstatement) * Hōki 3 (772), January 3: Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}}
== References == * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟}}, *Shoku Nihongi (Volume 1)*, {{ill|Kodansha|ja|講談社}}, Kodansha Academic Library, 1992. * Ujitani Mitsuru, *Shoku Nihongi (Volume 2)*, Kodansha, Kodansha Academic Library, 1992. * Ujitani Mitsuru, *Shoku Nihongi (Volume 3)*, Kodansha, Kodansha Academic Library, 1995.

== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}}
== References == * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟}}, *Shoku Nihongi (Volume 1)*, {{ill|Kodansha|ja|講談社}}, Kodansha Academic Library, 1992. * Ujitani Mitsuru, *Shoku Nihongi (Volume 2)*, Kodansha, Kodansha Academic Library, 1992. * Ujitani Mitsuru, *Shoku Nihongi (Volume 3)*, Kodansha, Kodansha Academic Library, 1995.

[[ja:%E6%B2%B3%E5%86%85%E7%8E%8B_(%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E6%99%82%E4%BB%A3%E5%BE%8C%E6%9C%9F)]][[Category:Two pages]]
'''Kawachi Ō''' (河内王, dates of birth and death unknown) was a {{ill|Prince of Japan|ja|日本の王 (皇族)}} during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. He held the rank of {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正五位下}} and served as Governor of {{ill|Tango Province|ja|丹後国}}.
== Career == In the court of [[Emperor Shōmu]] during the ninth year of the {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} era (737), Kawachi Ō was directly promoted from no rank to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下}}.
In the second year of the {{ill|Tenpyō Hōji|ja|天平宝字}} era (758), during the enthronement of Emperor {{ill|Junnin|ja|淳仁天皇}}, Kawachi Ō was dispatched to perform rituals at the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}} alongside {{ill|Nakatomi no Ikemori|ja|中臣池守}} and others. At the time, his official position was {{ill|Head of the Left Bureau of Imperial Attendants|ja|左大舎人頭}}.<ref>『続日本紀』天平宝字2年8月19日条</ref>
In the third year of the Tenpyō Hōji era (759), he received his first promotion in 22 years to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ltt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上}}. In the fourth year (760), he was appointed as {{ill|Assistant Minister of Justice|ja|義部大輔}}, and in the sixth year (762), he became Governor of Tango Province. However, he appears to have been implicated in the {{ill|Fujiwara no Nakamaro Rebellion|ja|藤原仲麻呂の乱}} in the eighth year (764), resulting in the loss of his rank and position.
In the first year of the {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀}} era (770), upon the enthronement of Emperor {{ill|Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇}}, he was reinstated to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. In the third year (772), he was promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade.
== Official Record == Based on the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}: * {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} 9 (737), September 28: Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (direct promotion) * Tenpyō Hōji 2 (758), August 19: Left Bureau of Imperial Attendants (current position) * Tenpyō Hōji 3 (759), June 16: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade * Tenpyō Hōji 4 (760), January 16: Assistant Minister of Justice * Tenpyō Hōji 6 (762), January 9: Governor of Tango Province * Date unknown: Rank and position forfeited * {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀}} 1 (770), October 1: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (reinstatement) * Hōki 3 (772), January 3: Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}}
== References == * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟}}, *Shoku Nihongi (Volume 1)*, {{ill|Kodansha|ja|講談社}}, Kodansha Academic Library, 1992. * Ujitani Mitsuru, *Shoku Nihongi (Volume 2)*, Kodansha, Kodansha Academic Library, 1992. * Ujitani Mitsuru, *Shoku Nihongi (Volume 3)*, Kodansha, Kodansha Academic Library, 1995.

== Official Record == Based on the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}: * {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} 9 (737), September 28: Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (direct promotion) * Tenpyō Hōji 2 (758), August 19: Left Bureau of Imperial Attendants (current position) * Tenpyō Hōji 3 (759), June 16: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade * Tenpyō Hōji 4 (760), January 16: Assistant Minister of Justice * Tenpyō Hōji 6 (762), January 9: Governor of Tango Province * Date unknown: Rank and position forfeited * {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀}} 1 (770), October 1: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (reinstatement) * Hōki 3 (772), January 3: Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade

[[ja:%E6%B2%B3%E5%86%85%E7%8E%8B_(%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E6%99%82%E4%BB%A3%E5%BE%8C%E6%9C%9F)]][[Category:Two pages]]
'''Kawachi Ō''' (河内王, dates of birth and death unknown) was a {{ill|Prince of Japan|ja|日本の王 (皇族)}} during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. He held the rank of {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正五位下}} and served as Governor of {{ill|Tango Province|ja|丹後国}}.
== Career == In the court of [[Emperor Shōmu]] during the ninth year of the {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} era (737), Kawachi Ō was directly promoted from no rank to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下}}.
In the second year of the {{ill|Tenpyō Hōji|ja|天平宝字}} era (758), during the enthronement of Emperor {{ill|Junnin|ja|淳仁天皇}}, Kawachi Ō was dispatched to perform rituals at the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}} alongside {{ill|Nakatomi no Ikemori|ja|中臣池守}} and others. At the time, his official position was {{ill|Head of the Left Bureau of Imperial Attendants|ja|左大舎人頭}}.<ref>『続日本紀』天平宝字2年8月19日条</ref>
In the third year of the Tenpyō Hōji era (759), he received his first promotion in 22 years to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ltt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上}}. In the fourth year (760), he was appointed as {{ill|Assistant Minister of Justice|ja|義部大輔}}, and in the sixth year (762), he became Governor of Tango Province. However, he appears to have been implicated in the {{ill|Fujiwara no Nakamaro Rebellion|ja|藤原仲麻呂の乱}} in the eighth year (764), resulting in the loss of his rank and position.
In the first year of the {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀}} era (770), upon the enthronement of Emperor {{ill|Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇}}, he was reinstated to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. In the third year (772), he was promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade.
== Official Record == Based on the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}: * {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} 9 (737), September 28: Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (direct promotion) * Tenpyō Hōji 2 (758), August 19: Left Bureau of Imperial Attendants (current position) * Tenpyō Hōji 3 (759), June 16: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade * Tenpyō Hōji 4 (760), January 16: Assistant Minister of Justice * Tenpyō Hōji 6 (762), January 9: Governor of Tango Province * Date unknown: Rank and position forfeited * {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀}} 1 (770), October 1: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (reinstatement) * Hōki 3 (772), January 3: Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}}
== References == * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟}}, *Shoku Nihongi (Volume 1)*, {{ill|Kodansha|ja|講談社}}, Kodansha Academic Library, 1992. * Ujitani Mitsuru, *Shoku Nihongi (Volume 2)*, Kodansha, Kodansha Academic Library, 1992. * Ujitani Mitsuru, *Shoku Nihongi (Volume 3)*, Kodansha, Kodansha Academic Library, 1995.

[[ja:%E6%B2%B3%E5%86%85%E7%8E%8B_(%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E6%99%82%E4%BB%A3%E5%BE%8C%E6%9C%9F)]][[Category:Two pages]]
'''Kawachi Ō''' (河内王, dates of birth and death unknown) was a {{ill|Prince of Japan|ja|日本の王 (皇族)}} during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. He held the rank of {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正五位下}} and served as Governor of {{ill|Tango Province|ja|丹後国}}.
== Career == In the court of [[Emperor Shōmu]] during the ninth year of the {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} era (737), Kawachi Ō was directly promoted from no rank to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下}}.
In the second year of the {{ill|Tenpyō Hōji|ja|天平宝字}} era (758), during the enthronement of Emperor {{ill|Junnin|ja|淳仁天皇}}, Kawachi Ō was dispatched to perform rituals at the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}} alongside {{ill|Nakatomi no Ikemori|ja|中臣池守}} and others. At the time, his official position was {{ill|Head of the Left Bureau of Imperial Attendants|ja|左大舎人頭}}.<ref>『続日本紀』天平宝字2年8月19日条</ref>
In the third year of the Tenpyō Hōji era (759), he received his first promotion in 22 years to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ltt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上}}. In the fourth year (760), he was appointed as {{ill|Assistant Minister of Justice|ja|義部大輔}}, and in the sixth year (762), he became Governor of Tango Province. However, he appears to have been implicated in the {{ill|Fujiwara no Nakamaro Rebellion|ja|藤原仲麻呂の乱}} in the eighth year (764), resulting in the loss of his rank and position.
In the first year of the {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀}} era (770), upon the enthronement of Emperor {{ill|Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇}}, he was reinstated to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. In the third year (772), he was promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade.
== Official Record == Based on the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}: * {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} 9 (737), September 28: Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (direct promotion) * Tenpyō Hōji 2 (758), August 19: Left Bureau of Imperial Attendants (current position) * Tenpyō Hōji 3 (759), June 16: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade * Tenpyō Hōji 4 (760), January 16: Assistant Minister of Justice * Tenpyō Hōji 6 (762), January 9: Governor of Tango Province * Date unknown: Rank and position forfeited * {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀}} 1 (770), October 1: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (reinstatement) * Hōki 3 (772), January 3: Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}}
== References == * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟}}, *Shoku Nihongi (Volume 1)*, {{ill|Kodansha|ja|講談社}}, Kodansha Academic Library, 1992. * Ujitani Mitsuru, *Shoku Nihongi (Volume 2)*, Kodansha, Kodansha Academic Library, 1992. * Ujitani Mitsuru, *Shoku Nihongi (Volume 3)*, Kodansha, Kodansha Academic Library, 1995.

In the second year of the {{ill|Tenpyō Hōji|ja|天平宝字}} era (758), during the enthronement of Emperor {{ill|Junnin|ja|淳仁天皇}}, Kawachi Ō was dispatched to perform rituals at the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}} alongside {{ill|Nakatomi no Ikemori|ja|中臣池守}} and others. At the time, his official position was {{ill|Head of the Left Bureau of Imperial Attendants|ja|左大舎人頭}}.<ref>『続日本紀』天平宝字2年8月19日条</ref>
In the third year of the Tenpyō Hōji era (759), he received his first promotion in 22 years to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ltt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上}}. In the fourth year (760), he was appointed as {{ill|Assistant Minister of Justice|ja|義部大輔}}, and in the sixth year (762), he became Governor of Tango Province. However, he appears to have been implicated in the {{ill|Fujiwara no Nakamaro Rebellion|ja|藤原仲麻呂の乱}} in the eighth year (764), resulting in the loss of his rank and position.
In the first year of the {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀}} era (770), upon the enthronement of Emperor {{ill|Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇}}, he was reinstated to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. In the third year (772), he was promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade.
== Official Record == Based on the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}: * {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} 9 (737), September 28: Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (direct promotion) * Tenpyō Hōji 2 (758), August 19: Left Bureau of Imperial Attendants (current position) * Tenpyō Hōji 3 (759), June 16: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade * Tenpyō Hōji 4 (760), January 16: Assistant Minister of Justice * Tenpyō Hōji 6 (762), January 9: Governor of Tango Province * Date unknown: Rank and position forfeited * {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀}} 1 (770), October 1: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (reinstatement) * Hōki 3 (772), January 3: Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}}

'''Kawachi Ō''' (河内王, dates of birth and death unknown) was a {{ill|Prince of Japan|ja|日本の王 (皇族)}} during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. He held the rank of {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正五位下}} and served as Governor of {{ill|Tango Province|ja|丹後国}}.

In the second year of the {{ill|Tenpyō Hōji|ja|天平宝字}} era (758), during the enthronement of Emperor {{ill|Junnin|ja|淳仁天皇}}, Kawachi Ō was dispatched to perform rituals at the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}} alongside {{ill|Nakatomi no Ikemori|ja|中臣池守}} and others. However, he appears to have been implicated in the {{ill|Fujiwara no Nakamaro Rebellion|ja|藤原仲麻呂の乱}} in the eighth year (764), resulting in the loss of his rank and position.
In the first year of the {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀}} era (770), upon the enthronement of Emperor {{ill|Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇}}, he was reinstated to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. In the third year (772), he was promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade.

[[ja:%E6%B2%B3%E5%86%85%E7%8E%8B_(%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E6%99%82%E4%BB%A3%E5%BE%8C%E6%9C%9F)]][[Category:Two pages]]
'''Kawachi Ō''' (河内王, dates of birth and death unknown) was a {{ill|Prince of Japan|ja|日本の王 (皇族)}} during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. He held the rank of {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正五位下}} and served as Governor of {{ill|Tango Province|ja|丹後国}}.
== Career == In the court of [[Emperor Shōmu]] during the ninth year of the {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} era (737), Kawachi Ō was directly promoted from no rank to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下}}.
In the second year of the {{ill|Tenpyō Hōji|ja|天平宝字}} era (758), during the enthronement of Emperor {{ill|Junnin|ja|淳仁天皇}}, Kawachi Ō was dispatched to perform rituals at the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}} alongside {{ill|Nakatomi no Ikemori|ja|中臣池守}} and others. At the time, his official position was {{ill|Head of the Left Bureau of Imperial Attendants|ja|左大舎人頭}}.<ref>『続日本紀』天平宝字2年8月19日条</ref>
In the third year of the Tenpyō Hōji era (759), he received his first promotion in 22 years to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ltt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上}}. In the fourth year (760), he was appointed as {{ill|Assistant Minister of Justice|ja|義部大輔}}, and in the sixth year (762), he became Governor of Tango Province. However, he appears to have been implicated in the {{ill|Fujiwara no Nakamaro Rebellion|ja|藤原仲麻呂の乱}} in the eighth year (764), resulting in the loss of his rank and position.
In the first year of the {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀}} era (770), upon the enthronement of Emperor {{ill|Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇}}, he was reinstated to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. In the third year (772), he was promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade.
== Official Record == Based on the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}: * {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} 9 (737), September 28: Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (direct promotion) * Tenpyō Hōji 2 (758), August 19: Left Bureau of Imperial Attendants (current position) * Tenpyō Hōji 3 (759), June 16: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade * Tenpyō Hōji 4 (760), January 16: Assistant Minister of Justice * Tenpyō Hōji 6 (762), January 9: Governor of Tango Province * Date unknown: Rank and position forfeited * {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀}} 1 (770), October 1: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (reinstatement) * Hōki 3 (772), January 3: Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}}
== References == * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟}}, *Shoku Nihongi (Volume 1)*, {{ill|Kodansha|ja|講談社}}, Kodansha Academic Library, 1992. * Ujitani Mitsuru, *Shoku Nihongi (Volume 2)*, Kodansha, Kodansha Academic Library, 1992. * Ujitani Mitsuru, *Shoku Nihongi (Volume 3)*, Kodansha, Kodansha Academic Library, 1995.

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|正五位下}}" to "|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}"
revision.text:
[[ja:%E6%B2%B3%E5%86%85%E7%8E%8B_(%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E6%99%82%E4%BB%A3%E5%BE%8C%E6%9C%9F)]][[Category:Two pages]]
'''Kawachi Ō''' (河内王, dates of birth and death unknown) was a {{ill|Prince of Japan|ja|日本の王 (皇族)}} during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. He held the rank of {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} and served as Governor of {{ill|Tango Province|ja|丹後国}}.
== Career == In the court of [[Emperor Shōmu]] during the ninth year of the {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} era (737), Kawachi Ō was directly promoted from no rank to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}.
In the second year of the {{ill|Tenpyō Hōji|ja|天平宝字}} era (758), during the enthronement of Emperor {{ill|Junnin|ja|淳仁天皇}}, Kawachi Ō was dispatched to perform rituals at the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}} alongside {{ill|Nakatomi no Ikemori|ja|中臣池守}} and others. At the time, his official position was {{ill|Head of the Left Bureau of Imperial Attendants|ja|左大舎人頭}}.<ref>『続日本紀』天平宝字2年8月19日条</ref>
In the third year of the Tenpyō Hōji era (759), he received his first promotion in 22 years to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ltt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}. In the fourth year (760), he was appointed as {{ill|Assistant Minister of Justice|ja|義部大輔}}, and in the sixth year (762), he became Governor of Tango Province. However, he appears to have been implicated in the {{ill|Fujiwara no Nakamaro Rebellion|ja|藤原仲麻呂の乱}} in the eighth year (764), resulting in the loss of his rank and position.
In the first year of the {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀}} era (770), upon the enthronement of Emperor {{ill|Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇}}, he was reinstated to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. In the third year (772), he was promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade.
== Official Record == Based on the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}: * {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} 9 (737), September 28: Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (direct promotion) * Tenpyō Hōji 2 (758), August 19: Left Bureau of Imperial Attendants (current position) * Tenpyō Hōji 3 (759), June 16: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade * Tenpyō Hōji 4 (760), January 16: Assistant Minister of Justice * Tenpyō Hōji 6 (762), January 9: Governor of Tango Province * Date unknown: Rank and position forfeited * {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀}} 1 (770), October 1: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (reinstatement) * Hōki 3 (772), January 3: Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}}
== References == * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟}}, *Shoku Nihongi (Volume 1)*, {{ill|Kodansha|ja|講談社}}, Kodansha Academic Library, 1992. * Ujitani Mitsuru, *Shoku Nihongi (Volume 2)*, Kodansha, Kodansha Academic Library, 1992. * Ujitani Mitsuru, *Shoku Nihongi (Volume 3)*, Kodansha, Kodansha Academic Library, 1995.

[[ja:%E6%B2%B3%E5%86%85%E7%8E%8B_(%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E6%99%82%E4%BB%A3%E5%BE%8C%E6%9C%9F)]][[Category:Two pages]]
'''Kawachi Ō''' (河内王, dates of birth and death unknown) was a {{ill|Prince of Japan|ja|日本の王 (皇族)}} during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. He held the rank of {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} and served as Governor of {{ill|Tango Province|ja|丹後国}}.
== Career == In the court of [[Emperor Shōmu]] during the ninth year of the {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} era (737), Kawachi Ō was directly promoted from no rank to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}.
In the second year of the {{ill|Tenpyō Hōji|ja|天平宝字}} era (758), during the enthronement of Emperor {{ill|Junnin|ja|淳仁天皇}}, Kawachi Ō was dispatched to perform rituals at the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}} alongside {{ill|Nakatomi no Ikemori|ja|中臣池守}} and others. At the time, his official position was {{ill|Head of the Left Bureau of Imperial Attendants|ja|左大舎人頭}}.<ref>『続日本紀』天平宝字2年8月19日条</ref>
In the third year of the Tenpyō Hōji era (759), he received his first promotion in 22 years to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ltt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}. In the fourth year (760), he was appointed as {{ill|Assistant Minister of Justice|ja|義部大輔}}, and in the sixth year (762), he became Governor of Tango Province. However, he appears to have been implicated in the {{ill|Fujiwara no Nakamaro Rebellion|ja|藤原仲麻呂の乱}} in the eighth year (764), resulting in the loss of his rank and position.
In the first year of the {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀}} era (770), upon the enthronement of Emperor {{ill|Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇}}, he was reinstated to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. In the third year (772), he was promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade.
== Official Record == Based on the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}: * {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} 9 (737), September 28: Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (direct promotion) * Tenpyō Hōji 2 (758), August 19: Left Bureau of Imperial Attendants (current position) * Tenpyō Hōji 3 (759), June 16: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade * Tenpyō Hōji 4 (760), January 16: Assistant Minister of Justice * Tenpyō Hōji 6 (762), January 9: Governor of Tango Province * Date unknown: Rank and position forfeited * {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀}} 1 (770), October 1: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (reinstatement) * Hōki 3 (772), January 3: Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}}
== References == * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟}}, *Shoku Nihongi (Volume 1)*, {{ill|Kodansha|ja|講談社}}, Kodansha Academic Library, 1992. * Ujitani Mitsuru, *Shoku Nihongi (Volume 2)*, Kodansha, Kodansha Academic Library, 1992. * Ujitani Mitsuru, *Shoku Nihongi (Volume 3)*, Kodansha, Kodansha Academic Library, 1995.

[[ja:%E6%B2%B3%E5%86%85%E7%8E%8B_(%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E6%99%82%E4%BB%A3%E5%BE%8C%E6%9C%9F)]][[Category:Two pages]]
'''Kawachi Ō''' (河内王, dates of birth and death unknown) was a {{ill|Prince of Japan|ja|日本の王 (皇族)}} during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. He held the rank of {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} and served as Governor of {{ill|Tango Province|ja|丹後国}}.
== Career == In the court of [[Emperor Shōmu]] during the ninth year of the {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} era (737), Kawachi Ō was directly promoted from no rank to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}.
In the second year of the {{ill|Tenpyō Hōji|ja|天平宝字}} era (758), during the enthronement of Emperor {{ill|Junnin|ja|淳仁天皇}}, Kawachi Ō was dispatched to perform rituals at the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}} alongside {{ill|Nakatomi no Ikemori|ja|中臣池守}} and others. At the time, his official position was {{ill|Head of the Left Bureau of Imperial Attendants|ja|左大舎人頭}}.<ref>『続日本紀』天平宝字2年8月19日条</ref>
In the third year of the Tenpyō Hōji era (759), he received his first promotion in 22 years to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ltt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}. In the fourth year (760), he was appointed as {{ill|Assistant Minister of Justice|ja|義部大輔}}, and in the sixth year (762), he became Governor of Tango Province. However, he appears to have been implicated in the {{ill|Fujiwara no Nakamaro Rebellion|ja|藤原仲麻呂の乱}} in the eighth year (764), resulting in the loss of his rank and position.
In the first year of the {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀}} era (770), upon the enthronement of Emperor {{ill|Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇}}, he was reinstated to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. In the third year (772), he was promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade.
== Official Record == Based on the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}: * {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} 9 (737), September 28: Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (direct promotion) * Tenpyō Hōji 2 (758), August 19: Left Bureau of Imperial Attendants (current position) * Tenpyō Hōji 3 (759), June 16: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade * Tenpyō Hōji 4 (760), January 16: Assistant Minister of Justice * Tenpyō Hōji 6 (762), January 9: Governor of Tango Province * Date unknown: Rank and position forfeited * {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀}} 1 (770), October 1: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (reinstatement) * Hōki 3 (772), January 3: Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}}
== References == * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟}}, *Shoku Nihongi (Volume 1)*, {{ill|Kodansha|ja|講談社}}, Kodansha Academic Library, 1992. * Ujitani Mitsuru, *Shoku Nihongi (Volume 2)*, Kodansha, Kodansha Academic Library, 1992. * Ujitani Mitsuru, *Shoku Nihongi (Volume 3)*, Kodansha, Kodansha Academic Library, 1995.

[[ja:%E6%B2%B3%E5%86%85%E7%8E%8B_(%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E6%99%82%E4%BB%A3%E5%BE%8C%E6%9C%9F)]][[Category:Two pages]]
'''Kawachi Ō''' (河内王, dates of birth and death unknown) was a {{ill|Prince of Japan|ja|日本の王 (皇族)}} during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. He held the rank of {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} and served as Governor of {{ill|Tango Province|ja|丹後国}}.
== Career == In the court of [[Emperor Shōmu]] during the ninth year of the {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} era (737), Kawachi Ō was directly promoted from no rank to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}.
In the second year of the {{ill|Tenpyō Hōji|ja|天平宝字}} era (758), during the enthronement of Emperor {{ill|Junnin|ja|淳仁天皇}}, Kawachi Ō was dispatched to perform rituals at the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}} alongside {{ill|Nakatomi no Ikemori|ja|中臣池守}} and others. At the time, his official position was {{ill|Head of the Left Bureau of Imperial Attendants|ja|左大舎人頭}}.<ref>『続日本紀』天平宝字2年8月19日条</ref>
In the third year of the Tenpyō Hōji era (759), he received his first promotion in 22 years to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ltt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}. In the fourth year (760), he was appointed as {{ill|Assistant Minister of Justice|ja|義部大輔}}, and in the sixth year (762), he became Governor of Tango Province. However, he appears to have been implicated in the {{ill|Fujiwara no Nakamaro Rebellion|ja|藤原仲麻呂の乱}} in the eighth year (764), resulting in the loss of his rank and position.
In the first year of the {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀}} era (770), upon the enthronement of Emperor {{ill|Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇}}, he was reinstated to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. In the third year (772), he was promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade.
== Official Record == Based on the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}: * {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} 9 (737), September 28: Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (direct promotion) * Tenpyō Hōji 2 (758), August 19: Left Bureau of Imperial Attendants (current position) * Tenpyō Hōji 3 (759), June 16: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade * Tenpyō Hōji 4 (760), January 16: Assistant Minister of Justice * Tenpyō Hōji 6 (762), January 9: Governor of Tango Province * Date unknown: Rank and position forfeited * {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀}} 1 (770), October 1: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (reinstatement) * Hōki 3 (772), January 3: Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade

[[ja:%E6%B2%B3%E5%86%85%E7%8E%8B_(%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E6%99%82%E4%BB%A3%E5%BE%8C%E6%9C%9F)]][[Category:Two pages]]
'''Kawachi Ō''' (河内王, dates of birth and death unknown) was a {{ill|Prince of Japan|ja|日本の王 (皇族)}} during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. He held the rank of {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} and served as Governor of {{ill|Tango Province|ja|丹後国}}.
== Career == In the court of [[Emperor Shōmu]] during the ninth year of the {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} era (737), Kawachi Ō was directly promoted from no rank to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}.
In the second year of the {{ill|Tenpyō Hōji|ja|天平宝字}} era (758), during the enthronement of Emperor {{ill|Junnin|ja|淳仁天皇}}, Kawachi Ō was dispatched to perform rituals at the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}} alongside {{ill|Nakatomi no Ikemori|ja|中臣池守}} and others. At the time, his official position was {{ill|Head of the Left Bureau of Imperial Attendants|ja|左大舎人頭}}.<ref>『続日本紀』天平宝字2年8月19日条</ref>
In the third year of the Tenpyō Hōji era (759), he received his first promotion in 22 years to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ltt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}. In the fourth year (760), he was appointed as {{ill|Assistant Minister of Justice|ja|義部大輔}}, and in the sixth year (762), he became Governor of Tango Province. However, he appears to have been implicated in the {{ill|Fujiwara no Nakamaro Rebellion|ja|藤原仲麻呂の乱}} in the eighth year (764), resulting in the loss of his rank and position.
In the first year of the {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀}} era (770), upon the enthronement of Emperor {{ill|Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇}}, he was reinstated to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. In the third year (772), he was promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade.
== Official Record == Based on the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}: * {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} 9 (737), September 28: Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (direct promotion) * Tenpyō Hōji 2 (758), August 19: Left Bureau of Imperial Attendants (current position) * Tenpyō Hōji 3 (759), June 16: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade * Tenpyō Hōji 4 (760), January 16: Assistant Minister of Justice * Tenpyō Hōji 6 (762), January 9: Governor of Tango Province * Date unknown: Rank and position forfeited * {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀}} 1 (770), October 1: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (reinstatement) * Hōki 3 (772), January 3: Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}}
== References == * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟}}, *Shoku Nihongi (Volume 1)*, {{ill|Kodansha|ja|講談社}}, Kodansha Academic Library, 1992. * Ujitani Mitsuru, *Shoku Nihongi (Volume 2)*, Kodansha, Kodansha Academic Library, 1992. * Ujitani Mitsuru, *Shoku Nihongi (Volume 3)*, Kodansha, Kodansha Academic Library, 1995.

revision.text:
[[ja:%E6%B2%B3%E5%86%85%E7%8E%8B_(%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E6%99%82%E4%BB%A3%E5%BE%8C%E6%9C%9F)]][[Category:Two pages]]
'''Kawachi Ō''' (河内王, dates of birth and death unknown) was a {{ill|Prince of Japan|ja|日本の王 (皇族)}} during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. He held the rank of {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} and served as Governor of {{ill|Tango Province|ja|丹後国}}.
== Career == In the court of [[Emperor Shōmu]] during the ninth year of the {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} era (737), Kawachi Ō was directly promoted from no rank to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}.
In the second year of the {{ill|Tenpyō Hōji|ja|天平宝字}} era (758), during the enthronement of Emperor {{ill|Junnin|ja|淳仁天皇}}, Kawachi Ō was dispatched to perform rituals at the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}} alongside {{ill|Nakatomi no Ikemori|ja|中臣池守}} and others. At the time, his official position was {{ill|Head of the Left Bureau of Imperial Attendants|ja|左大舎人頭}}.<ref>『続日本紀』天平宝字2年8月19日条</ref>
In the third year of the Tenpyō Hōji era (759), he received his first promotion in 22 years to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ltt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}. In the fourth year (760), he was appointed as {{ill|Assistant Minister of Justice|ja|義部大輔}}, and in the sixth year (762), he became Governor of Tango Province. However, he appears to have been implicated in the {{ill|Fujiwara no Nakamaro Rebellion|ja|藤原仲麻呂の乱}} in the eighth year (764), resulting in the loss of his rank and position.
In the first year of the {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀}} era (770), upon the enthronement of Emperor {{ill|Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇}}, he was reinstated to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. In the third year (772), he was promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade.
== Official Record == Based on the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}: * {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} 9 (737), September 28: Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (direct promotion) * Tenpyō Hōji 2 (758), August 19: Left Bureau of Imperial Attendants (current position) * Tenpyō Hōji 3 (759), June 16: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade * Tenpyō Hōji 4 (760), January 16: Assistant Minister of Justice * Tenpyō Hōji 6 (762), January 9: Governor of Tango Province * Date unknown: Rank and position forfeited * {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀}} 1 (770), October 1: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (reinstatement) * Hōki 3 (772), January 3: Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}}
== References == * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟}}, *Shoku Nihongi (Volume 1)*, {{ill|Kodansha|ja|講談社}}, Kodansha Academic Library, 1992. * Ujitani Mitsuru, *Shoku Nihongi (Volume 2)*, Kodansha, Kodansha Academic Library, 1992. * Ujitani Mitsuru, *Shoku Nihongi (Volume 3)*, Kodansha, Kodansha Academic Library, 1995.

[[ja:%E6%B2%B3%E5%86%85%E7%8E%8B_(%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E6%99%82%E4%BB%A3%E5%BE%8C%E6%9C%9F)]][[Category:Two pages]]
'''Kawachi Ō''' (河内王, dates of birth and death unknown) was a {{ill|Prince of Japan|ja|日本の王 (皇族)}} during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. He held the rank of {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} and served as Governor of {{ill|Tango Province|ja|丹後国}}.
== Career == In the court of [[Emperor Shōmu]] during the ninth year of the {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} era (737), Kawachi Ō was directly promoted from no rank to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}.
In the second year of the {{ill|Tenpyō Hōji|ja|天平宝字}} era (758), during the enthronement of Emperor {{ill|Junnin|ja|淳仁天皇}}, Kawachi Ō was dispatched to perform rituals at the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}} alongside {{ill|Nakatomi no Ikemori|ja|中臣池守}} and others. At the time, his official position was {{ill|Head of the Left Bureau of Imperial Attendants|ja|左大舎人頭}}.<ref>『続日本紀』天平宝字2年8月19日条</ref>
In the third year of the Tenpyō Hōji era (759), he received his first promotion in 22 years to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ltt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}. In the fourth year (760), he was appointed as {{ill|Assistant Minister of Justice|ja|義部大輔}}, and in the sixth year (762), he became Governor of Tango Province. However, he appears to have been implicated in the {{ill|Fujiwara no Nakamaro Rebellion|ja|藤原仲麻呂の乱}} in the eighth year (764), resulting in the loss of his rank and position.
In the first year of the {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀}} era (770), upon the enthronement of Emperor {{ill|Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇}}, he was reinstated to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. In the third year (772), he was promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade.
== Official Record == Based on the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}: * {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} 9 (737), September 28: Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (direct promotion) * Tenpyō Hōji 2 (758), August 19: Left Bureau of Imperial Attendants (current position) * Tenpyō Hōji 3 (759), June 16: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade * Tenpyō Hōji 4 (760), January 16: Assistant Minister of Justice * Tenpyō Hōji 6 (762), January 9: Governor of Tango Province * Date unknown: Rank and position forfeited * {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀}} 1 (770), October 1: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (reinstatement) * Hōki 3 (772), January 3: Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade

revision.text:
[[ja:%E6%B2%B3%E5%86%85%E7%8E%8B_(%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E6%99%82%E4%BB%A3%E5%BE%8C%E6%9C%9F)]][[Category:Two pages]]
'''Kawachi Ō''' (河内王, dates of birth and death unknown) was a {{ill|Prince of Japan|ja|日本の王 (皇族)}} during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. He held the rank of {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} and served as Governor of {{ill|Tango Province|ja|丹後国}}.
== Career == In the court of [[Emperor Shōmu]] during the ninth year of the {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} era (737), Kawachi Ō was directly promoted from no rank to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}.
In the second year of the {{ill|Tenpyō Hōji|ja|天平宝字}} era (758), during the enthronement of Emperor {{ill|Junnin|ja|淳仁天皇}}, Kawachi Ō was dispatched to perform rituals at the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}} alongside {{ill|Nakatomi no Ikemori|ja|中臣池守}} and others. At the time, his official position was {{ill|Head of the Left Bureau of Imperial Attendants|ja|左大舎人頭}}.<ref>『続日本紀』天平宝字2nd year 8月19日条</ref>
In the third year of the Tenpyō Hōji era (759), he received his first promotion in 22 years to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ltt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}. In the fourth year (760), he was appointed as {{ill|Assistant Minister of Justice|ja|義部大輔}}, and in the sixth year (762), he became Governor of Tango Province. However, he appears to have been implicated in the {{ill|Fujiwara no Nakamaro Rebellion|ja|藤原仲麻呂の乱}} in the eighth year (764), resulting in the loss of his rank and position.
In the first year of the {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀}} era (770), upon the enthronement of Emperor {{ill|Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇}}, he was reinstated to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. In the third year (772), he was promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade.
== Official Record == Based on the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}: * {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} 9 (737), September 28: Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (direct promotion) * Tenpyō Hōji 2 (758), August 19: Left Bureau of Imperial Attendants (current position) * Tenpyō Hōji 3 (759), June 16: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade * Tenpyō Hōji 4 (760), January 16: Assistant Minister of Justice * Tenpyō Hōji 6 (762), January 9: Governor of Tango Province * Date unknown: Rank and position forfeited * {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀}} 1 (770), October 1: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (reinstatement) * Hōki 3 (772), January 3: Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade
== Notes ==
{{Reflist|2}}
== References == * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟}}, *Shoku Nihongi (Volume 1)*, {{ill|Kodansha|ja|講談社}}, Kodansha Academic Library, 1992. * Ujitani Mitsuru, *Shoku Nihongi (Volume 2)*, Kodansha, Kodansha Academic Library, 1992. * Ujitani Mitsuru, *Shoku Nihongi (Volume 3)*, Kodansha, Kodansha Academic Library, 1995.

'''Kawachi Ō''' (河内王, dates of birth and death unknown) was a {{ill|Prince of Japan|ja|日本の王 (皇族)|12=draft}} during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代|12=draft}}. He held the rank of {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=draft}} and served as Governor of {{ill|Tango Province|ja|丹後国|12=draft}}.
== Career == In the court of [[Emperor Shōmu]] during the ninth year of the {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平|12=draft}} era (737), Kawachi Ō was directly promoted from no rank to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=draft}}.
In the second year of the {{ill|Tenpyō Hōji|ja|天平宝字|12=draft}} era (758), during the enthronement of Emperor {{ill|Junnin|ja|淳仁天皇|12=draft}}, Kawachi Ō was dispatched to perform rituals at the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft}} alongside {{ill|Nakatomi no Ikemori|ja|中臣池守|12=draft}} and others. At the time, his official position was {{ill|Head of the Left Bureau of Imperial Attendants|ja|左大舎人頭|12=draft}}.<ref>『続日本紀』天平宝字2nd year 8月19日条</ref>
In the third year of the Tenpyō Hōji era (759), he received his first promotion in 22 years to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ltt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=draft}}. In the fourth year (760), he was appointed as {{ill|Assistant Minister of Justice|ja|義部大輔|12=draft}}, and in the sixth year (762), he became Governor of Tango Province. However, he appears to have been implicated in the {{ill|Fujiwara no Nakamaro Rebellion|ja|藤原仲麻呂の乱|12=draft}} in the eighth year (764), resulting in the loss of his rank and position.
In the first year of the {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀|12=draft}} era (770), upon the enthronement of Emperor {{ill|Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇|12=draft}}, he was reinstated to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. In the third year (772), he was promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade.
== Official Record == Based on the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀|12=draft}}: * {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平|12=draft}} 9 (737), September 28: Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (direct promotion) * Tenpyō Hōji 2 (758), August 19: Left Bureau of Imperial Attendants (current position) * Tenpyō Hōji 3 (759), June 16: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade * Tenpyō Hōji 4 (760), January 16: Assistant Minister of Justice * Tenpyō Hōji 6 (762), January 9: Governor of Tango Province * Date unknown: Rank and position forfeited * {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀|12=draft}} 1 (770), October 1: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (reinstatement) * Hōki 3 (772), January 3: Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade
== Notes ==
{{Reflist|2}}
== References == * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟|12=draft}}, *Shoku Nihongi (Volume 1)*, {{ill|Kodansha|ja|講談社|12=draft}}, Kodansha Academic Library, 1992. * Ujitani Mitsuru, *Shoku Nihongi (Volume 2)*, Kodansha, Kodansha Academic Library, 1992. * Ujitani Mitsuru, *Shoku Nihongi (Volume 3)*, Kodansha, Kodansha Academic Library, 1995.

revision.text:
'''河内王'''（かわちおう、生没年不詳）は、[[奈良時代]]の[[皇族]]。[[官位]]は[[正五位|正五位下]]・[[丹後国#国司|丹後守]]。
== 経歴 == [[聖武天皇|聖武朝]]の[[天平]]9年（[[737年]]）[[无位]]から[[従五位|従五位下]]に直叙される。
[[天平宝字]]2年（[[758年]]）[[淳仁天皇]]の[[即位]]に際して、[[奉幣]]を行うために、[[散位]]・[[中臣池守]]らとともに[[伊勢神宮|伊勢太神宮]]に派遣された（この時の[[官職]]は[[大舎人寮|左大舎人頭]]）<ref>『続日本紀』天平宝字2年8月19日条</ref>。天平宝字3年（[[759年]]）22年振りに昇叙されて従五位上になると、天平宝字4年（[[760年]]）[[刑部省|義部大輔]]、天平宝字6年（[[762年]]）[[丹後国#国司|丹後守]]に任ぜられている。しかし、天平宝字8年（[[764年]]）に発生した[[藤原仲麻呂の乱]]に[[連座]]したらしく[[官位]]を剥奪される。
[[宝亀]]元年（[[770年]]）[[光仁天皇]]の即位に伴って従五位上に復されると、宝亀3年（[[772年]]）には[[正五位|正五位下]]に叙せられた。
== 官歴 == 『[[続日本紀]]』による。 *[[天平]]9年（[[737年]]） 9月28日：[[従五位|従五位下]]（[[蔭位|直叙]]） *[[天平宝字]]2年（[[758年]]） 8月19日：見[[大舎人寮|左大舎人頭]] *天平宝字3年（[[759年]]） 6月16日：従五位上 *天平宝字4年（[[760年]]） 正月16日：[[刑部省|義部大輔]] *天平宝字6年（[[762年]]） 正月9日：[[丹後国#国司|丹後守]] *時期不詳：[[官位]]剥奪 *[[宝亀]]元年（[[770年]]） 10月1日：従五位上（復本位） *宝亀3年（[[772年]]） 正月3日：[[正五位|正五位下]]
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}}
== 参考文献 == *[[宇治谷孟]]『続日本紀 （上）』[[講談社]]〈[[講談社学術文庫]]〉、1992年 *宇治谷孟『続日本紀 （中）』講談社〈講談社学術文庫〉、1992年 *宇治谷孟『続日本紀 （下）』講談社〈講談社学術文庫〉、1995年
{{DEFAULTSORT:かわちおう}} [[Category:日本の王 (皇族)]] [[Category:奈良時代の男性皇族]] [[Category:正五位下受位者]] [[Category:8世紀生]] [[Category:8世紀没]]

'''河内王'''（かわちおう、生没年不詳）は、[[奈良時代]]の[[皇族]]。[[官位]]は[[正五位|正五位下]]・[[丹後国#国司|丹後守]]。
== 経歴 == [[聖武天皇|聖武朝]]の[[天平]]9年（[[737年]]）[[无位]]から[[従五位|従五位下]]に直叙される。
[[天平宝字]]2年（[[758年]]）[[淳仁天皇]]の[[即位]]に際して、[[奉幣]]を行うために、[[散位]]・[[中臣池守]]らとともに[[伊勢神宮|伊勢太神宮]]に派遣された（この時の[[官職]]は[[大舎人寮|左大舎人頭]]）<ref>『続日本紀』天平宝字2年8月19日条</ref>。天平宝字3年（[[759年]]）22年振りに昇叙されて従五位上になると、天平宝字4年（[[760年]]）[[刑部省|義部大輔]]、天平宝字6年（[[762年]]）[[丹後国#国司|丹後守]]に任ぜられている。しかし、天平宝字8年（[[764年]]）に発生した[[藤原仲麻呂の乱]]に[[連座]]したらしく[[官位]]を剥奪される。
[[宝亀]]元年（[[770年]]）[[光仁天皇]]の即位に伴って従五位上に復されると、宝亀3年（[[772年]]）には[[正五位|正五位下]]に叙せられた。
== 官歴 == 『[[続日本紀]]』による。 *[[天平]]9年（[[737年]]） 9月28日：[[従五位|従五位下]]（[[蔭位|直叙]]） *[[天平宝字]]2年（[[758年]]） 8月19日：見[[大舎人寮|左大舎人頭]] *天平宝字3年（[[759年]]） 6月16日：従五位上 *天平宝字4年（[[760年]]） 正月16日：[[刑部省|義部大輔]] *天平宝字6年（[[762年]]） 正月9日：[[丹後国#国司|丹後守]] *時期不詳：[[官位]]剥奪 *[[宝亀]]元年（[[770年]]） 10月1日：従五位上（復本位） *宝亀3年（[[772年]]） 正月3日：[[正五位|正五位下]]
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}}
== 参考文献 == *[[宇治谷孟]]『続日本紀 （上）』[[講談社]]〈[[講談社学術文庫]]〉、1992年 *宇治谷孟『続日本紀 （中）』講談社〈講談社学術文庫〉、1992年 *宇治谷孟『続日本紀 （下）』講談社〈講談社学術文庫〉、1995年

'''Kawachi Ō''' (河内王, dates of birth and death unknown) was a {{ill|Prince of Japan|ja|日本の王 (皇族)|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}. He held the Rank of {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} and served as Governor of {{ill|Tango Province|ja|丹後国|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}.
== Career == In the court of [[Emperor Shōmu]] during the ninth year of the {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} era (737), Kawachi Ō was directly promoted from no Rank to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}.
In the second year of the {{ill|Tenpyō Hōji|ja|天平宝字|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} era (758), during the enthronement of Emperor {{ill|Junnin|ja|淳仁天皇|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, Kawachi Ō was dispatched to perform rituals at the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} alongside {{ill|Nakatomi no Ikemori|ja|中臣池守|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} and others. At the time, his official position was {{ill|Head of the Left Bureau of Imperial Attendants|ja|左大舎人頭|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}.<ref>『続日本紀』天平宝字2nd year 8月19日条</ref>
In the third year of the Tenpyō Hōji era (759), he received his first promotion in 22 years to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ltt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}. In the fourth year (760), he was appointed as {{ill|Assistant Minister of Justice|ja|義部大輔|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, and in the sixth year (762), he became Governor of Tango Province. However, he appears to have been implicated in the {{ill|Fujiwara no Nakamaro Rebellion|ja|藤原仲麻呂の乱|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} in the eighth year (764), resulting in the loss of his Rank and position.
In the first year of the {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} era (770), upon the enthronement of Emperor {{ill|Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, he was reinstated to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. In the third year (772), he was promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade.
== Official Record == Based on the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}: * {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 9 (737), September 28: Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (direct promotion) * Tenpyō Hōji 2 (758), August 19: Left Bureau of Imperial Attendants (current position) * Tenpyō Hōji 3 (759), June 16: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade * Tenpyō Hōji 4 (760), January 16: Assistant Minister of Justice * Tenpyō Hōji 6 (762), January 9: Governor of Tango Province * Date unknown: Rank and position forfeited * {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 1 (770), October 1: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (reinstatement) * Hōki 3 (772), January 3: Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade
== Notes ==
{{Reflist|2}}
== References == * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, *Shoku Nihongi (Volume 1)*, {{ill|Kodansha|ja|講談社|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, Kodansha Academic Library, 1992. * Ujitani Mitsuru, *Shoku Nihongi (Volume 2)*, Kodansha, Kodansha Academic Library, 1992. * Ujitani Mitsuru, *Shoku Nihongi (Volume 3)*, Kodansha, Kodansha Academic Library, 1995.
{{translated page|ja|河内王 (奈良時代後期)|version=86558792|comment=Imported full ja history}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:Kawachi no Ōkimi (Late Nara period)}}

He held the Rank of {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=}} and served as Governor of {{ill|Tango Province|ja|丹後国|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tango Province|qq=}}.

'''Kawachi Ō''' (河内王, dates of birth and death unknown) was a {{ill|Prince of Japan|ja|日本の王 (皇族)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince of Japan|qq=}} during the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]]. He held the Rank of {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=}} and served as Governor of [[:en:Tango Province|Tango Province]].
== Career == In the court of [[Emperor Shōmu]] during the ninth year of the [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]] era (737), Kawachi Ō was directly promoted from no Rank to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}}.
In the second year of the [[:en:Tenpyō-hōji|Tenpyō-hōji]] era (758), during the enthronement of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Junnin|Junnin]], Kawachi Ō was dispatched to perform rituals at the [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]] alongside {{ill|Nakatomi no Ikemori|ja|中臣池守|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Ikemori|qq=}} and others. At the time, his official position was {{ill|Head of the Left Bureau of Imperial Attendants|ja|左大舎人頭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Head of the Left Bureau of Imperial Attendants|qq=}}.<ref>『続日本紀』天平宝字2nd year 8月19日条</ref>
In the third year of the Tenpyō Hōji era (759), he received his first promotion in 22 years to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ltt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}}. In the fourth year (760), he was appointed as {{ill|Assistant Minister of Justice|ja|義部大輔|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Assistant Minister of Justice|qq=}}, and in the sixth year (762), he became Governor of Tango Province. However, he appears to have been implicated in the [[:en:Fujiwara no Nakamaro Rebellion|Fujiwara no Nakamaro Rebellion]] in the eighth year (764), resulting in the loss of his Rank and position.
In the first year of the [[:en:Hōki|Hōki]] era (770), upon the enthronement of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Kōnin|Kōnin]], he was reinstated to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. In the third year (772), he was promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade.
== Official Record == Based on the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]]: * [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]] 9 (737), September 28: Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (direct promotion) * Tenpyō Hōji 2 (758), August 19: Left Bureau of Imperial Attendants (current position) * Tenpyō Hōji 3 (759), June 16: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade * Tenpyō Hōji 4 (760), January 16: Assistant Minister of Justice * Tenpyō Hōji 6 (762), January 9: Governor of Tango Province * Date unknown: Rank and position forfeited * [[:en:Hōki|Hōki]] 1 (770), October 1: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (reinstatement) * Hōki 3 (772), January 3: Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade

revision.text:
'''Kawachi Ō''' (河内王, dates of birth and death unknown) was a {{ill|Prince of Japan|ja|日本の王 (皇族)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince of Japan|qq=}} during the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]]. He held the Rank of {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=}} and served as Governor of [[:en:Tango Province|Tango Province]].
== Career == In the court of [[Emperor Shōmu]] during the ninth year of the [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]] era (737), Kawachi Ō was directly promoted from no Rank to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}}.
In the second year of the [[:en:Tenpyō-hōji|Tenpyō-hōji]] era (758), during the enthronement of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Junnin|Junnin]], Kawachi Ō was dispatched to perform rituals at the [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]] alongside {{ill|Nakatomi no Ikemori|ja|中臣池守|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Ikemori|qq=}} and others. At the time, his official position was {{ill|Head of the Left Bureau of Imperial Attendants|ja|左大舎人頭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Head of the Left Bureau of Imperial Attendants|qq=}}.<ref>『続日本紀』天平宝字2nd year 8月19日条</ref>
In the third year of the Tenpyō Hōji era (759), he received his first promotion in 22 years to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ltt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}}. In the fourth year (760), he was appointed as {{ill|Assistant Minister of Justice|ja|義部大輔|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Assistant Minister of Justice|qq=}}, and in the sixth year (762), he became Governor of Tango Province. However, he appears to have been implicated in the [[:en:Fujiwara no Nakamaro Rebellion|Fujiwara no Nakamaro Rebellion]] in the eighth year (764), resulting in the loss of his Rank and position.
In the first year of the [[:en:Hōki|Hōki]] era (770), upon the enthronement of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Kōnin|Kōnin]], he was reinstated to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. In the third year (772), he was promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade.
== Official Record == Based on the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]]: * [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]] 9 (737), September 28: Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (direct promotion) * Tenpyō Hōji 2 (758), August 19: Left Bureau of Imperial Attendants (current position) * Tenpyō Hōji 3 (759), June 16: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade * Tenpyō Hōji 4 (760), January 16: Assistant Minister of Justice * Tenpyō Hōji 6 (762), January 9: Governor of Tango Province * Date unknown: Rank and position forfeited * [[:en:Hōki|Hōki]] 1 (770), October 1: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (reinstatement) * Hōki 3 (772), January 3: Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade
== Notes ==

== References == * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujitani Mitsuru|qq=}}, *Shoku Nihongi (Volume 1)*, [[:en:Kodansha|Kodansha]], Kodansha Academic Library, 1992. * Ujitani Mitsuru, *Shoku Nihongi (Volume 2)*, Kodansha, Kodansha Academic Library, 1992. * Ujitani Mitsuru, *Shoku Nihongi (Volume 3)*, Kodansha, Kodansha Academic Library, 1995.

revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:27, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:06, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kawachi Ō''' (河内王, dates of birth and death unknown) was a {{ill|Prince of Japan|ja|日本の王 (皇族)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince of Japan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} during the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]]. He held the Rank of {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}} and served as Governor of [[:en:Tango Province|Tango Province]].
== Career == In the court of [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmu]] during the ninth year of the [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]] era (737), Kawachi Ō was directly promoted from no Rank to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}.
In the second year of the [[:en:Tenpyō-hōji|Tenpyō-hōji]] era (758), during the enthronement of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Junnin|Junnin]], Kawachi Ō was dispatched to perform rituals at the [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]] alongside {{ill|Nakatomi no Ikemori|ja|中臣池守|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Ikemori|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and others. At the time, his official position was {{ill|Head of the Left Bureau of Imperial Attendants|ja|左大舎人頭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Head of the Left Bureau of Imperial Attendants|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大舎人寮}}.<ref>『続日本紀』天平宝字2nd year 8月19日条</ref>
In the third year of the Tenpyō Hōji era (759), he received his first promotion in 22 years to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ltt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. In the fourth year (760), he was appointed as {{ill|Assistant Minister of Justice|ja|義部大輔|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Assistant Minister of Justice|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and in the sixth year (762), he became Governor of Tango Province. However, he appears to have been implicated in the [[:en:Fujiwara no Nakamaro Rebellion|Fujiwara no Nakamaro Rebellion]] in the eighth year (764), resulting in the loss of his Rank and position.
In the first year of the [[:en:Hōki|Hōki]] era (770), upon the enthronement of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Kōnin|Kōnin]], he was reinstated to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. In the third year (772), he was promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade.
== Official Record == Based on the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]]: * [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]] 9 (737), September 28: Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (direct promotion) * Tenpyō Hōji 2 (758), August 19: Left Bureau of Imperial Attendants (current position) * Tenpyō Hōji 3 (759), June 16: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade * Tenpyō Hōji 4 (760), January 16: Assistant Minister of Justice * Tenpyō Hōji 6 (762), January 9: Governor of Tango Province * Date unknown: Rank and position forfeited * [[:en:Hōki|Hōki]] 1 (770), October 1: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (reinstatement) * Hōki 3 (772), January 3: Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade
== Notes ==
{{Reflist|2}}
== References == * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujitani Mitsuru|qq=}}, *Shoku Nihongi (Volume 1)*, [[:en:Kodansha|Kodansha]], Kodansha Academic Library, 1992. * Ujitani Mitsuru, *Shoku Nihongi (Volume 2)*, Kodansha, Kodansha Academic Library, 1992. * Ujitani Mitsuru, *Shoku Nihongi (Volume 3)*, Kodansha, Kodansha Academic Library, 1995.

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17209039]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:17, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:27, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:06, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kawachi Ō''' (河内王, dates of birth and death unknown) was a {{ill|Prince of Japan|ja|日本の王 (皇族)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince of Japan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} during the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]]. He held the Rank of {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}} and served as Governor of [[:en:Tango Province|Tango Province]].
== Career == In the court of [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmu]] during the ninth year of the [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]] era (737), Kawachi Ō was directly promoted from no Rank to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}.
In the second year of the [[:en:Tenpyō-hōji|Tenpyō-hōji]] era (758), during the enthronement of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Junnin|Junnin]], Kawachi Ō was dispatched to perform rituals at the [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]] alongside {{ill|Nakatomi no Ikemori|ja|中臣池守|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Ikemori|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and others. At the time, his official position was {{ill|Head of the Left Bureau of Imperial Attendants|ja|左大舎人頭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Head of the Left Bureau of Imperial Attendants|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大舎人寮}}.<ref>『続日本紀』天平宝字2nd year 8月19日条</ref>
In the third year of the Tenpyō Hōji era (759), he received his first promotion in 22 years to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ltt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. In the fourth year (760), he was appointed as {{ill|Assistant Minister of Justice|ja|義部大輔|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Assistant Minister of Justice|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and in the sixth year (762), he became Governor of Tango Province. However, he appears to have been implicated in the [[:en:Fujiwara no Nakamaro Rebellion|Fujiwara no Nakamaro Rebellion]] in the eighth year (764), resulting in the loss of his Rank and position.
In the first year of the [[:en:Hōki|Hōki]] era (770), upon the enthronement of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Kōnin|Kōnin]], he was reinstated to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. In the third year (772), he was promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade.
== Official Record == Based on the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]]: * [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]] 9 (737), September 28: Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (direct promotion) * Tenpyō Hōji 2 (758), August 19: Left Bureau of Imperial Attendants (current position) * Tenpyō Hōji 3 (759), June 16: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade * Tenpyō Hōji 4 (760), January 16: Assistant Minister of Justice * Tenpyō Hōji 6 (762), January 9: Governor of Tango Province * Date unknown: Rank and position forfeited * [[:en:Hōki|Hōki]] 1 (770), October 1: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (reinstatement) * Hōki 3 (772), January 3: Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade
== Notes ==
{{Reflist|2}}
== References == * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujitani Mitsuru|qq=}}, *Shoku Nihongi (Volume 1)*, [[:en:Kodansha|Kodansha]], Kodansha Academic Library, 1992. * Ujitani Mitsuru, *Shoku Nihongi (Volume 2)*, Kodansha, Kodansha Academic Library, 1992. * Ujitani Mitsuru, *Shoku Nihongi (Volume 3)*, Kodansha, Kodansha Academic Library, 1995.

At the time, his official position was {{ill|Head of the Left Bureau of Imperial Attendants|ja|左大舎人頭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Head of the Left Bureau of Imperial Attendants|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大舎人寮}}.<ref>『続日本紀』天平宝字2nd year 8月19日条</ref>
In the third year of the Tenpyō Hōji era (759), he received his first promotion in 22 years to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ltt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. However, he appears to have been implicated in the [[:en:Fujiwara no Nakamaro Rebellion|Fujiwara no Nakamaro Rebellion]] in the eighth year (764), resulting in the loss of his Rank and position.

In the second year of the [[:en:Tenpyō-hōji|Tenpyō-hōji]] era (758), during the enthronement of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Junnin|Junnin]], Kawachi Ō was dispatched to perform rituals at the [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]] alongside {{ill|Nakatomi no Ikemori|ja|中臣池守|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Ikemori|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135491609}} and others. At the time, his official position was {{ill|Head of the Left Bureau of Imperial Attendants|ja|左大舎人頭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Head of the Left Bureau of Imperial Attendants|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大舎人寮}}.<ref>『続日本紀』天平宝字2nd year 8月19日条</ref>
In the third year of the Tenpyō Hōji era (759), he received his first promotion in 22 years to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ltt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. In the fourth year (760), he was appointed as {{ill|Assistant Minister of Justice|ja|義部大輔|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Assistant Minister of Justice|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and in the sixth year (762), he became Governor of Tango Province. However, he appears to have been implicated in the [[:en:Fujiwara no Nakamaro Rebellion|Fujiwara no Nakamaro Rebellion]] in the eighth year (764), resulting in the loss of his Rank and position.
In the first year of the [[:en:Hōki|Hōki]] era (770), upon the enthronement of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Kōnin|Kōnin]], he was reinstated to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. In the third year (772), he was promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade.
== Official Record == Based on the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]]: * [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]] 9 (737), September 28: Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (direct promotion) * Tenpyō Hōji 2 (758), August 19: Left Bureau of Imperial Attendants (current position) * Tenpyō Hōji 3 (759), June 16: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade * Tenpyō Hōji 4 (760), January 16: Assistant Minister of Justice * Tenpyō Hōji 6 (762), January 9: Governor of Tango Province * Date unknown: Rank and position forfeited * [[:en:Hōki|Hōki]] 1 (770), October 1: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (reinstatement) * Hōki 3 (772), January 3: Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade
