<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:河内王 (奈良時代後期)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17209039]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:17, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:27, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:06, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kawachi Ō''' (河内王, dates of birth and death unknown) was a {{ill|Prince of Japan|ja|日本の王 (皇族)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince of Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} during the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]]. He held the Rank of {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}} and served as Governor of [[:en:Tango Province|Tango Province]].
== Career == In the court of [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmu]] during the ninth year of the [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]] era (737), Kawachi Ō was directly promoted from no Rank to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}.
In the second year of the [[:en:Tenpyō-hōji|Tenpyō-hōji]] era (758), during the enthronement of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Junnin|Junnin]], Kawachi Ō was dispatched to perform rituals at the [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]] alongside {{ill|Nakatomi no Ikemori|ja|中臣池守|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Ikemori|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491609}} and others. At the time, his official position was {{ill|Head of the Left Bureau of Imperial Attendants|ja|左大舎人頭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Head of the Left Bureau of Imperial Attendants|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大舎人寮}}.<ref>『続日本紀』天平宝字2nd year 8月19日条</ref>
In the third year of the Tenpyō Hōji era (759), he received his first promotion in 22 years to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ltt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. In the fourth year (760), he was appointed as {{ill|Assistant Minister of Justice|ja|義部大輔|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Assistant Minister of Justice|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and in the sixth year (762), he became Governor of Tango Province. However, he appears to have been implicated in the [[:en:Fujiwara no Nakamaro Rebellion|Fujiwara no Nakamaro Rebellion]] in the eighth year (764), resulting in the loss of his Rank and position.
In the first year of the [[:en:Hōki|Hōki]] era (770), upon the enthronement of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Kōnin|Kōnin]], he was reinstated to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. In the third year (772), he was promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade.
== Official Record == Based on the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]]: * [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]] 9 (737), September 28: Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (direct promotion) * Tenpyō Hōji 2 (758), August 19: Left Bureau of Imperial Attendants (current position) * Tenpyō Hōji 3 (759), June 16: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade * Tenpyō Hōji 4 (760), January 16: Assistant Minister of Justice * Tenpyō Hōji 6 (762), January 9: Governor of Tango Province * Date unknown: Rank and position forfeited * [[:en:Hōki|Hōki]] 1 (770), October 1: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (reinstatement) * Hōki 3 (772), January 3: Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade

In the second year of the [[:en:Tenpyō-hōji|Tenpyō-hōji]] era (758), during the enthronement of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Junnin|Junnin]], Kawachi Ō was dispatched to perform rituals at the [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]] alongside {{ill|Nakatomi no Ikemori|ja|中臣池守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Ikemori|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491609}} and others. At the time, his official position was {{ill|Head of the Left Bureau of Imperial Attendants|ja|左大舎人頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Head of the Left Bureau of Imperial Attendants|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大舎人寮}}.<ref>『続日本紀』天平宝字2nd year 8月19日条</ref>
In the third year of the Tenpyō Hōji era (759), he received his first promotion in 22 years to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ltt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. In the fourth year (760), he was appointed as {{ill|Assistant Minister of Justice|ja|義部大輔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Assistant Minister of Justice|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and in the sixth year (762), he became Governor of Tango Province. However, he appears to have been implicated in the [[:en:Fujiwara no Nakamaro Rebellion|Fujiwara no Nakamaro Rebellion]] in the eighth year (764), resulting in the loss of his Rank and position.
In the first year of the [[:en:Hōki|Hōki]] era (770), upon the enthronement of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Kōnin|Kōnin]], he was reinstated to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. In the third year (772), he was promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade.
== Official Record == Based on the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]]: * [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]] 9 (737), September 28: Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (direct promotion) * Tenpyō Hōji 2 (758), August 19: Left Bureau of Imperial Attendants (current position) * Tenpyō Hōji 3 (759), June 16: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade * Tenpyō Hōji 4 (760), January 16: Assistant Minister of Justice * Tenpyō Hōji 6 (762), January 9: Governor of Tango Province * Date unknown: Rank and position forfeited * [[:en:Hōki|Hōki]] 1 (770), October 1: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (reinstatement) * Hōki 3 (772), January 3: Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade

== Career == In the court of [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmu]] during the ninth year of the [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]] era (737), Kawachi Ō was directly promoted from no Rank to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}.
In the second year of the [[:en:Tenpyō-hōji|Tenpyō-hōji]] era (758), during the enthronement of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Junnin|Junnin]], Kawachi Ō was dispatched to perform rituals at the [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]] alongside {{ill|Nakatomi no Ikemori|ja|中臣池守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Ikemori|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491609}} and others. At the time, his official position was {{ill|Head of the Left Bureau of Imperial Attendants|ja|左大舎人頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Head of the Left Bureau of Imperial Attendants|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大舎人寮}}.<ref>『続日本紀』天平宝字2nd year 8月19日条</ref>
In the third year of the Tenpyō Hōji era (759), he received his first promotion in 22 years to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ltt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. In the fourth year (760), he was appointed as {{ill|Assistant Minister of Justice|ja|義部大輔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Assistant Minister of Justice|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and in the sixth year (762), he became Governor of Tango Province. However, he appears to have been implicated in the [[:en:Fujiwara no Nakamaro Rebellion|Fujiwara no Nakamaro Rebellion]] in the eighth year (764), resulting in the loss of his Rank and position.
In the first year of the [[:en:Hōki|Hōki]] era (770), upon the enthronement of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Kōnin|Kōnin]], he was reinstated to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. In the third year (772), he was promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade.
== Official Record == Based on the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]]: * [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]] 9 (737), September 28: Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (direct promotion) * Tenpyō Hōji 2 (758), August 19: Left Bureau of Imperial Attendants (current position) * Tenpyō Hōji 3 (759), June 16: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade * Tenpyō Hōji 4 (760), January 16: Assistant Minister of Justice * Tenpyō Hōji 6 (762), January 9: Governor of Tango Province * Date unknown: Rank and position forfeited * [[:en:Hōki|Hōki]] 1 (770), October 1: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (reinstatement) * Hōki 3 (772), January 3: Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade
== Notes ==
{{Reflist|2}}
== References == * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujitani Mitsuru|qq=}}, *Shoku Nihongi (Volume 1)*, [[:en:Kodansha|Kodansha]], Kodansha Academic Library, 1992. * Ujitani Mitsuru, *Shoku Nihongi (Volume 2)*, Kodansha, Kodansha Academic Library, 1992. * Ujitani Mitsuru, *Shoku Nihongi (Volume 3)*, Kodansha, Kodansha Academic Library, 1995.
{{translated page|ja|河内王 (奈良時代後期)|version=86558792|comment=Imported full ja history}}

In the third year of the Tenpyō Hōji era (759), he received his first promotion in 22 years to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ltt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. In the fourth year (760), he was appointed as {{ill|Assistant Minister of Justice|ja|義部大輔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Assistant Minister of Justice|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and in the sixth year (762), he became Governor of Tango Province. However, he appears to have been implicated in the {{ill|Fujiwara no Nakamaro Rebellion|en|Fujiwara no Nakamaro Rebellion|WD=Q11623017|lt=Fujiwara no Nakamaro Rebellion|1=Fujiwara no Nakamaro Rebellion}} in the eighth year (764), resulting in the loss of his Rank and position.
In the first year of the {{ill|Hōki|en|Hōki|WD=Q18520676|lt=Hōki|1=Hōki}} era (770), upon the enthronement of Emperor {{ill|Emperor Kōnin|en|Emperor Kōnin|WD=Q958662|lt=Kōnin|1=Emperor Kōnin}}, he was reinstated to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. In the third year (772), he was promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade.

In the fourth year (760), he was appointed as {{ill|Assistant Minister of Justice|ja|義部大輔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Assistant Minister of Justice|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and in the sixth year (762), he became Governor of Tango Province. However, he appears to have been implicated in the {{ill|Fujiwara no Nakamaro Rebellion|en|Fujiwara no Nakamaro Rebellion|qid=Q11623017|lt=Fujiwara no Nakamaro Rebellion|1=Fujiwara no Nakamaro Rebellion}} in the eighth year (764), resulting in the loss of his Rank and position.
In the first year of the {{ill|Hōki|en|Hōki|qid=Q18520676|lt=Hōki|1=Hōki}} era (770), upon the enthronement of Emperor {{ill|Emperor Kōnin|en|Emperor Kōnin|qid=Q958662|lt=Kōnin|1=Emperor Kōnin}}, he was reinstated to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. In the third year (772), he was promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade.
== Official Record == Based on the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|en|Shoku Nihongi|qid=Q611399|lt=Shoku Nihongi|1=Shoku Nihongi}}: * {{ill|Tenpyō|en|Tenpyō|qid=Q1199879|lt=Tenpyō|1=Tenpyō}} 9 (737), September 28: Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (direct promotion) * Tenpyō Hōji 2 (758), August 19: Left Bureau of Imperial Attendants (current position) * Tenpyō Hōji 3 (759), June 16: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade * Tenpyō Hōji 4 (760), January 16: Assistant Minister of Justice * Tenpyō Hōji 6 (762), January 9: Governor of Tango Province * Date unknown: Rank and position forfeited * {{ill|Hōki|en|Hōki|qid=Q18520676|lt=Hōki|1=Hōki}} 1 (770), October 1: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (reinstatement) * Hōki 3 (772), January 3: Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade
